page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 32 virtual CPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz, the CPU includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance is configured with 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-speed local instance store capabilities. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps and is optimized for both network and Elastic Block Store performance. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates a distinct performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth scale to average levels, supported by multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests. LLM inference tasks on small to medium models perform at average speeds, though performance drops to poor for large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5dn.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated compute, substantial DDR4 memory, and fast local NVMe storage without the premium of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. This makes it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation tasks that benefit from local high-speed storage and balanced network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 3.1 GHz, the CPU supports AVX-512 instructions. While single-core benchmarks show weaker performance, multi-core and memory bandwidth tests consistently rank in the average tier. With a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps and no integrated GPU, this instance is designed for balanced workloads. It is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and software compilation where local high-speed storage and reliable network throughput are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for balanced multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and optimized network performance.
